title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s are definitely disruptive, especially when they're affecting multiple team members.

To help us narrow this down, could you walk us through the exact steps you're taking when the 403 appears? For example:

- Are you logging in fresh, or are you already authenticated when it happens?
- Is it a specific dashboard page/URL, or any page within the dashboard?
- Does refreshing the page after a 403 sometimes make it load successfully?

Anything you can share about the sequence of actions leading up to the error would be really helpful.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yeah, I'm already logged in when it happens. It's not like my session is expiring or anything — I can see I'm still logged in. It happens on pretty much any page, not just one specific dashboard. And yes, if I refresh after getting a 403, sometimes it works on the next try. Actually that's kind of my workaround right now, I just keep refreshing until it loads. It feels like maybe 1 out of 3 times I get the error? I was thinking it might be some kind of network issue on our end but I'm not sure.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for those details — that's really helpful. The refresh-sometimes-fixes-it pattern and the roughly 1-in-3 frequency are good clues.

Could you share some environment details? Specifically:

- **Browser and version** you're using (e.g., Chrome 124, Firefox 126)
- **OS** (e.g., Windows 11, macOS Sonoma)
- **TaskFlow version** or deployment (if you know it — might be visible in a footer or settings page)
- Whether your team members seeing the same issue are on similar setups or a mix of browsers/OSes

This will help us figure out whether the 403s are tied to a specific client environment or something happening server-side.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm using Chrome, not sure of the exact version but it's pretty recent — I have auto-updates on. I'm on Windows 11. As for TaskFlow version, I think it says v2.3.1 somewhere in the footer? And my coworkers who are seeing it are on a mix of stuff — one's on Mac with Chrome, another uses Firefox on Windows. So I don't think it's a browser thing. Actually, now that I think about it, the people on my team who are having this issue were all recently moved to a new 'analyst' role. I'm not sure if that's related but figured I'd mention it.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden errors for users assigned to new 'analyst' role","problem":"Multiple team members are receiving intermittent 403 Forbidden errors when accessing any TaskFlow dashboard page. The errors occur approximately 1 in 3 page loads. Users remain authenticated — sessions are not expiring — but authorization is inconsistently denied. Refreshing the page sometimes resolves the error. All affected users were recently moved to a new 'analyst' role.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The new 'analyst' role has a permissions or RBAC configuration issue that causes inconsistent authorization. The intermittent nature (works on refresh, ~33% failure rate) suggests either: (a) permission definitions are inconsistently propagated across multiple application servers behind a load balancer, (b) a caching layer (e.g., role/permission cache) is stale or racing with the recent role change, or (c) the analyst role's permissions were incompletely configured and a fallback/default-deny path is hit non-deterministically.","reproduction_steps":["Assign a user to the 'analyst' role","Log in as that user","Navigate to any TaskFlow dashboard page","Observe that approximately 1 in 3 page loads returns a 403 Forbidden error","Refresh the page — it may load successfully on retry"],"environment":"TaskFlow v2.3.1. Reproduced on Windows 11 (Chrome, Firefox) and macOS (Chrome). Not browser- or OS-specific.","severity":"high","impact":"All users recently assigned to the 'analyst' role are affected. They can partially work around the issue by refreshing, but roughly a third of page loads fail, significantly disrupting productivity. Multiple team members are impacted.","recommended_fix":"1. Inspect the permissions/RBAC configuration for the 'analyst' role — verify it grants dashboard access consistently. 2. Check whether the application runs behind a load balancer with multiple instances and whether role/permission data is synchronized across all instances. 3. Investigate any permission caching layer — look for stale caches or race conditions following role assignments. 4. Compare the analyst role's permission set against a known-working role (e.g., the role these users had before) to identify missing or inconsistent grants.","proposed_test_case":"Assign a test user to the 'analyst' role, then make 20+ sequential authenticated requests to various dashboard endpoints and assert that none return 403. Repeat across multiple server instances if load-balanced.","information_gaps":["Exact Chrome version (likely not relevant given cross-browser reproduction)","Server-side logs or request IDs for the 403 responses (would confirm whether specific servers or cache states correlate with failures)","Whether the 403 response body contains any additional detail beyond the status code"]}
